_note: PROPOSED corrected manifest (research/editions.proposed.json). Web-verified June 2026. See research/CORRECTIONS.md for every change and research/sources.bib for citations. Originals in data/editions.json left untouched.

editions.title: Alice's Adventures Under Ground
editions.note: Carroll's handwritten, self-illustrated manuscript (37 drawings), gifted to Alice Liddell 1864. BL Add MS 46700; facsimile = Gutenberg #19002.

editions.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland (first edition)
editions.note: 42 Tenniel illustrations engraved by the Dalziel Brothers, Macmillan. 1865 printing suppressed over quality; published edition dated 1866; suppressed sheets sold to Appleton, NY.

editions.title: The Nursery Alice (colorized Tenniel)
editions.note: 20 Tenniel plates enlarged & coloured (Tenniel-supervised), engraved/colour-printed by Edmund Evans. Cover designed & coloured by E. Gertrude Thomson. Printed 1889, issued 1890. CORRECTED from earlier 'colorist: e-gertrude-thomson'.

editions.title: Alice — illustrated by W. H. Walker
editions.note: John Lane / The Bodley Head, 8 colour plates. Illustrator identity disputed (see people/william-h-walker.md).

editions.title: Alice — illustrated by Arthur Rackham
editions.note: Heinemann: 13 colour plates + line drawings. Gutenberg #28885.

editions.title: La Aventuroj de Alicio en Mirlando — illustrated by Brinsley Le Fanu
editions.note: The ESPERANTO Alice, British Esperanto Association, 1910 (CORRECTED — not an English edition).

editions.title: Alice — Tenniel re-colored by Harry Theaker
editions.note: Macmillan commissioned Theaker (Tenniel-approved) to colour 16 Tenniel compositions; the blue dress becomes standard.

editions.title: Alice — "Emily Overnell"
editions.note: Everett & Co.; single frontispiece. Illustrator UNVERIFIED — name read from a signature, no biographical record. Not a confirmed pseudonym.

editions.title: Alice — illustrated by Alice B. Woodward
editions.note: G. Bell & Sons, Queen's Treasure Series, 16 colour plates.

editions.title: Alice — illustrated by Gwynedd Hudson
editions.note: Hodder & Stoughton, 12 colour plates; also a Boots the Chemist issue. (Artist lifespan uncertain.)

editions.title: Alice in Wonderland & Through the Looking-Glass — illustrated by Harry Rountree
editions.note: Collins Clear-Type Press, combined edition. Rountree also did a 1908 Thomas Nelson Alice (NOT Nister).

editions.title: Alice — illustrated by Bessie Pease Gutmann
editions.note: CORRECTED: first published 1907 by Dodge Publishing Co., NY. The '1933' was a later J. Coker & Co. (London) reissue. Consider renaming id to gutmann-1907.
